title: EKP Final Acceptance Output
description: Codex final verdict comparing delivered code against the ORIGINAL build.md (not the plan). The requirement_doc path in the project memory file is the authoritative spec - gate verification must ultimately fall back to it. Strict-mode: every object has additionalProperties=false and every property is required (use defaults for not-applicable).

delivered.description: True iff EVERY gate in build.md frontmatter has status=met with verified evidence.

gate_results.description: One entry per gate from build.md frontmatter. No gate may be skipped.

gate_description.description: Verbatim from build.md frontmatter for traceability.

evidence.description: Concrete proof: file:line, test output, command execution. 'Looks correct' is NOT evidence.

concerns.description: Latent risks even if status=met (e.g., 'works but no error handling for malformed input'). Empty string if none.

summary.description: <=300 words: overall delivery assessment for the human reviewer.

blockers.description: Specific reasons to NOT ship. Empty iff delivered=true.

non_blocking_followups.description: Improvements suggested but not required for this delivery.

recommendation.description: ship=delivery accepted; fix_blockers_and_re_run=address listed blockers and re-run Accept; redesign=plan is fundamentally off, go back to Plan phase.

project_memory_used.description: True if the final acceptance agent successfully read the project memory file (PROJECT_CONTEXT.md); false if missing/unreadable. Required for audit trail.

project_memory_hash.description: SHA-256 hex digest of the project memory file contents as read during Accept. null if the file was missing. Required so post-run audit can confirm the acceptance verdict was computed against a known memory snapshot.

requirement_doc_alignment.description: verified_aligned = gate_results were explicitly verified against the requirement_doc path in project memory (the authoritative spec), and every gate marked met actually satisfies the authoritative spec. unavailable = requirement_doc path was missing/unreadable, so final authoritative-spec verification was not possible. This is the final fallback verification - intermediate phase reviews do not substitute for it.
